contentList.content: You are proficient with this weapon and can use it with your Spellcasting Ability if it is higher than your Strength.
Enchantments are unlocked based on the owner's proficiency bonus (which increases at character level 1, 5 and 9).
As you grow more powerful, <LSTag Type="Passive" Tooltip="MOD_ScalingWeapons_TOOLTIP_ONLY">so does this item</LSTag>.
Targets hit by this extremely unsanitary tool receive an additional [1].
Unlocks <LSTag Type="Spell" Tooltip="Target_MAG_Surgeon_Anaesthesia_extra">Anaesthesia</LSTag> when wielded together with any other magical surgical weapons.
Oozes have <LSTag Tooltip="Disadvantage">Disadvantage</LSTag> on <LSTag Tooltip="AttackRoll">Attack Rolls</LSTag> against the wielder.
It's hard to be discreet or charming when you smell like a latrine; Charisma -1, Stealth -2
Targets hit with this weapon might be <LSTag Type="Status" Tooltip="POISONED">Poisoned</LSTag>.
A strike that deals additional poison damage and has a chance to make your target <LSTag Type="Status" Tooltip="STINKING_CLOUD">Nauseous</LSTag>.
During the first turn of combat, if you are not dual-wielding, you can use a bonus action to make an attack.
When the wielder is wearing Corellon's Wrath, this weapon gains +1 to Attack Rolls and grants True Strike at the start of combat.
Lighter than most armour of its class yet providing similar protection, truly, Corellon would be proud.
When the wearer is wielding Larethian's Wrath they gain <LSTag Type="Spell" Tooltip="MOD_KatanaArmor_Target_CompelledDuel">Compelled Duel</LSTag>.
Elves and Half-Elves are proficient with this armor.
Mom's Cleaver gets +1 bonus to <LSTag Tooltip="AttackRoll">Attack Rolls</LSTag>.
Mom's Cleaver gains [1] but Dad's Axe curse is reduced to [2].
Wielding a second axe gives Dad's Axe +1 to Attack Rolls.
Wielding Mom's Cleaver together with Dad's Axe unlocks <LSTag Type="Spell" Tooltip="MOD_Target_Command_Container">Mother's Orders</LSTag>.
Be a good kid and listen to your mother, especially when she calls you for diner or sends you to your room.
Stop playing that game you always play and come to diner now!
You know what you did; don't even try to argue.
Wielding Dad's Axe together with Mom's Cleaver unlocks <LSTag Type="Spell" Tooltip="MOD_Target_EnsnaringStrike_2">You're Grounded!</LSTag>.
That should prevent you from sneaking out!
A Devil and his pet Warlock devised a ritual to steal the powers of a Great Old One. It went as well as one can imagine but, at least, the blade wasn't completely destroyed; unlike them.
Weapon attack that shoots a beam of crackling energy from your blade.
Increased range and the blast can now push the target.
Weapon attack that shoots a beam of crackling energy from your blade.

Enchantment +2.
Hadar's Bite: Deals an extra [1].
Hunger of Hadar.
Unlocked at level 5, improved at level 9.
As you grow <LSTag Type="Passive" Tooltip="MOD_ScalingWeapons_TOOLTIP_ONLY">more powerful</LSTag>, so does this weapon.
During combat, this weapon channels Hadar's eternal hunger and deals an extra [1]; increased by [2], as your connection to Hadar grows stronger.
+2 bonus to <LSTag Tooltip="AttackRoll">Attack Rolls</LSTag> made with this weapon.
Dragonborns and Draconic Sorcerers gain <LSTag Type="Passive" Tooltip="MOD_ArcaneVersatility_STR_TOOLTIP_ONLY">Arcane Versatility</LSTag>.
This weapon will grow <LSTag Type="Passive" Tooltip="MOD_ScalingWeapons_TOOLTIP_ONLY">more powerful</LSTag> over time.
Dragonborns and Draconic Sorcerers gain <LSTag Type="Passive" Tooltip="MOD_ArcaneVersatility_STR_TOOLTIP_ONLY">Arcane Versatility</LSTag>.
Only the strongest minds can resist the psychic backlash from this blade; Gnomes are immune to it and are proficient with this weapon.
Removes Psychic Backlash; unlocks <LSTag Type="Spell" Tooltip="Target_Smite_Wrathful">Wrathful Smite</LSTag>.
You gain a +[1] bonus to your jump distance during the first turn of combat.
While you are <LSTag Type="Status" Tooltip="DRUNK">Drunk</LSTag>, and not wearing heavy armour, all incoming damage is reduced by 2.

Gain <LSTag Tooltip="Advantage">Advantage</LSTag> on <LSTag Tooltip="Dexterity">Dexterity</LSTag> <LSTag Tooltip="AbilityCheck">Checks</LSTag> and <LSTag Tooltip="SavingThrow">Saving Throws</LSTag>, while standing in water.
As your hand touches the broken wood, you are overwhelmed by memories of raging battles, crushing storms and countless sea shanties sung by long dead pirates ... "Yo, Ho, Ho ..."

Yo, Ho, Ho (and a Bottle of Rum!). Nothing like a good sea shanty to start a party! While drunk, you are <LSTag Tooltip="MusicalInstrumentProficiency">Proficient</LSTag> with musical instruments and gain a 2d6 bonus to performance checks.
The words "property of Captain Cog-Swallow" are etched on the handle.

When <LSTag Type="Status" Tooltip="DRUNK">Drunk</LSTag>, the number you need to <LSTag Tooltip="AttackRoll">roll</LSTag> a <LSTag Tooltip="CriticalHit">Critical Hit</LSTag> while attacking is reduced by 1.
A rusty old helmet that, as its name implies, was used to puke in. You're not considering wearing it, right?
You're actually wearing it! Ugh ... so gross!!
You can barely stand but your aim is as true as ever. You gain +1d2 to <LSTag Tooltip="AttackRoll">Attack Rolls</LSTag> when <LSTag Type="Status" Tooltip="DRUNK">Drunk</LSTag>.

Getting <LSTag Type="Status" Tooltip="DRUNK">Drunk</LSTag> while wearing this helmet makes you sick. Once per turn, you can <LSTag Type="Spell" Tooltip="MOD_Zone_PukeBucket_Acid">puke on your enemies</LSTag>.
While not hindered by heavy armour, you can use a bonus action to attack with your <LSTag Type="Spell" Tooltip="MOD_Target_Bash_BA_Attack">Shield</LSTag>.
This attack is considered an Unarmed Strike for the purpose of its Attack Rolls. Its damage does not benefit from <LSTag Tooltip="MeleeWeaponAttack">Weapon Attacks</LSTag> or Unarmed Attacks bonuses.
Use your shield to push a creature up to [1] away from you and possibly <LSTag Type="Status" Tooltip="DAZED">Daze</LSTag> them.
This shield is light enough for its attack to benefit from <LSTag Type="Passive" Tooltip="FightingStyle_TwoWeaponFighting">Two-Weapon Fighting</LSTag>.
Because of its weight, this shield's attack cannot benefit from <LSTag Type="Passive" Tooltip="FightingStyle_TwoWeaponFighting">Two-Weapon Fighting</LSTag>.
While not hindered by heavy armour, you can use a bonus action to <LSTag Type="Spell" Tooltip="MOD_Target_Bash_Spiked_BA_Attack">Impale</LSTag> a target with those nasty-looking spikes.
This attack is considered an Unarmed Strike for the purpose of its Attack Rolls. Its damage does not benefit from <LSTag Tooltip="MeleeWeaponAttack">Weapon Attacks</LSTag> or Unarmed Attacks bonuses.

The shield burns for 6 turns and:
Sheds light in a [1] radius;
Gains a Shield Attack that deals [2];
Loses 1 Armor Class.
Sheds light in a [1] radius; unlocks <LSTag Type="Spell" Tooltip="MOD_FlamingShield_BA_Attack">Shield Attack</LSTag>; -1 AC.
As the hunter grows <LSTag Type="Passive" Tooltip="MOD_ScalingWeapons_TOOLTIP_ONLY">stronger</LSTag>, so does this weapon.
Bleeding Wound: Causes the target to become Ruptured, for 3 turns.

Enchantment +1.
Hunter's Strike: After a weapon attack you can use a bonus action to Attack Again.

Weapon attack that deals an additional [2]; can cause your target to become <LSTag Type="Status" Tooltip="CHILLED">Chilled</LSTag> and prevent it from taking <LSTag Type="ActionResource" Tooltip="ReactionActionPoint">Reactions</LSTag> for 1 turn.
After making a weapon attack you can use a bonus action to attack again, as long as you are not hindered by heavy armour or holding a shield.
Take [1] damage when moving. Every time it is triggered, the duration is reduced by 1 turn.
"One day I'll be a great Hero!"

Some say the most powerful thing in Faerun is a child's imagination and when you see them running around with their little wooden swords and their little wooden shields, it looks like it might just be true.

Affected entity becomes a symbol of heroism. It gains +2 Charisma and has <LSTag Tooltip="Advantage">Advantage</LSTag> on Charisma <LSTag Tooltip="AbilityCheck">Checks</LSTag> and <LSTag Tooltip="SavingThrow">Saving Throws</LSTag>.
A must-have for those hot summer days.

Deals additional damage to targets that need refreshing:
[1] to targets that are NOT wet;
[2] to targets that are burning.
A pale white birch staff twisted around a bright peridot shard. It is cool to the touch.
When standing in liquid water (or blood) you can teleport to any other body of water (or blood) that you can see. After using this ability you can make a weapon attack as a bonus action.
You will need time to attune to this staff's <LSTag Type="Passive" Tooltip="MOD_ScalingWeapons_TOOLTIP_ONLY">power</LSTag>.
A water whip that pulls a target [2] towards you. If successful, the target receives [1] and becomes <LSTag Type="Status" Tooltip="WET">Wet</LSTag> for [3] turns.
During combat, if you are standing in water, Water Whip only costs a bonus action to cast.
Sussur makes it hard to create sharp blades, but it compensates by releasing raw magic on a hit.
Incoming Spell Attacks power a <LSTag Type="Passive" Tooltip="MOD_Sussur_Wards_TOOLTIP_ONLY">Magical Ward</LSTag> of your choice.
Incoming spell attacks deal [1] to the attacker.

Sussur weapons absord magical energy directed at them to charge a Magical Ward. These wards last until a Spell Attack misses them. The size of their healing and damage dice increases to 1d6 at level 5 and 1d8 at level 9.
Swap place with any ally who is also wearing a Devilfoil Mask. After using this ability you need to wait 5 turns before you can use it again.
The energy of this weapon feels so unnatural, it will take <LSTag Type="Passive" Tooltip="MOD_ScalingWeapons_TOOLTIP_ONLY">time</LSTag> to master.
Soul Harvester: Turn the souls of your victims into soul coins.
Infernal Footsoldier: While wearing a Devilfoil Mask you gain Arcane Versatility.
Infernal Legionnaire: Being part of an Infernal Legion gives this weapon +1 bonus to Attack Rolls and ignites it.
Strike up to 3 targets, possibly setting them on fire.
When drunk patrons refuse to leave, call The Manager.
Make anyone in a [1] radius, <LSTag Type="Status" Tooltip="DRUNK">Drunk</LSTag>.
The Manager can be used in one of two ways:
Some patrons just don't know when to leave! Small chance to turn Undead creatures. Deals an extra [1].
They're eating the barley! Gain Advantage against Beasts and Monstrosities. Deals an extra [1].

Attack Rolls: -1d4;
Spell Save DC: -1;
Movement: -[1];
Disadvantage on all Ability Checks except Charisma.
If the wielder deals [1], [2], [3], [4] or [5], they become resistant to that damage type for 2 turns.

Melee and Thrown attacks deal an extra [1].
Melee hits may also Shock your target.
This weapon will return to your hand when thrown.
When holding the Fossilised Shell you gain Disarming Strike and +1 to Attack Rolls.
When wielding Lightning Jabber you become resistant to Lightning damage.
Ignore difficult terrain when you are <LSTag Type="Status" Tooltip="WET">Wet</LSTag>.
When you have 3 Adamantine items equipped, you gain another +1 Constitution and +1 to Saving Throws against spells.
When both Adamantine weapon and armour are equipped you gain +1 Constitution.
When both Adamantine weapon and armour are equipped, deal an extra [1] to targets that are <LSTag Type="Status" Tooltip="MAG_ATTACK_DEBUFF">Reeling</LSTag>.
Duergars have long understood that slaves only work as hard as you can hit them.
Hard Work can be used in one of two ways:

Weapon attack that deals Bludgeoning, Lightning and Psychic damage. It also <LSTag Type="Status" Tooltip="SHOCKING_GRASP">Shocks</LSTag> and <LSTag Type="Status" Tooltip="DISTRACTED">Distracts</LSTag> your target.
This blade is as heavy as a Maul; when you miss an attack, you deal [1] anyway.
On a hit with an unarmed or thrown attack, the wearer gains [1] Lightning Charges.
While imbued with Lightning Charges, you gain Shocking Grasp and attacks against metal constructs and foes wearing metal armour gain Advantage.
The recipe has been lost in time but the dye still works.
Combine this dye with an equipment item to alter the colours of fabric, leather and even metal.
This simple leather armour made of goat hide was enchanted by a travelling druid. A goatherd once helped her negotiate a strange maze of hedges, so she blessed all his goats in thanks.
Gnomes, Dwarves and Halflings gain [1] of movement.
Scored with slime and blood, it seems this leather armour couldn't save its previous owner. Although it may help the next.
When the wearer heals a creature, they both Disengage and won't trigger <LSTag Tooltip="OpportunityAttack"> Opportunity Attacks</LSTag>.
Embroidered with a simple maxim - 'Nature is the True State of the World' - this leather armor radiates a faint divine power.
I weaved in a little spell for my favourite little person. Love, Lenore.

Creature has a +1 bonus to <LSTag Tooltip="ArmourClass">Armour Class</LSTag>, <LSTag Tooltip="Advantage">Advantage</LSTag> on Dexterity <LSTag Tooltip="SavingThrow">Saving Throws</LSTag>, its <LSTag Tooltip="MovementSpeed">movement speed</LSTag> is increased by [1], and it can take one additional bonus action per turn.
Become <LSTag Type="Status" Tooltip="MOD_LITTLEHASTE">Hastened</LSTag>: gain a bonus action, become faster and gain +1 to <LSTag Tooltip="ArmourClass">Armour Class</LSTag>.
Dents, notches, and nicks add character to each plate of this armour - yet each blemish also speaks to those who tasted magical vengeance on the armour's behalf.
You can channel the magic of this armour in one of two ways:
Your melee attacks apply 1 turn of <LSTag Type="Status" Tooltip="MAG_THUNDER_REVERBERATION">Reverberation</LSTag> to your target.
When struck, those gauntlets pulse with energy.
Melee attack that deal an additional [1] per remaining turn of <LSTag Type="Status" Tooltip="MAG_ZOC_FORCE_CONDUIT">Force Conduit</LSTag>, to a maximum of [2].
Gain 1 turn of <LSTag Type="Status" Tooltip="MAG_ZOC_FORCE_CONDUIT">Force Conduit</LSTag> when taking Slashing, Piercing, or Bludgeoning damage.
Although tarnished by time, this helmet feels as sturdy as ever.
Gain 1 turn of <LSTag Type="Status" Tooltip="MAG_ZOC_FORCE_CONDUIT">Force Conduit</LSTag> when taking damage.

While having turns of <LSTag Type="Status" Tooltip="MAG_ZOC_FORCE_CONDUIT">Force Conduit</LSTag> the wearer is surrounded by a [1] wide invisible force field that is considered <LSTag Type="Status" Tooltip="DIFFICULT_TERRAIN">Difficult Terrain</LSTag>.
All entities affected by this aura have their <LSTag Tooltip="MovementSpeed">movement speed</LSTag> halved.

Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage against the affected entity is reduced by [1] per turn remaining.

If the entity takes damage while it has 5 or more turns remaining, it deals [2] to enemies in a [3] radius.
Drows are never as formidable as when they are swallowed by shadows.
Shadows cling to those gloves like honey on a spoon.
+1 bonus to <LSTag Tooltip="ArmourClass">Armour Class</LSTag> and <LSTag Tooltip="AttackRoll">Attack Rolls</LSTag> while obscured.
A powerful offence is sometimes still the best defence.
Merely holding the helmet makes your skin flush, filling you with determination and righteous fury.
Fire reveals the weaknesses of your enemies.
You gain a +[1] bonus to spell <LSTag Tooltip="AttackRoll">attack rolls</LSTag>.

Blind targets that fail a DC 10 Dexterity saving throw.
Deals an additional [1] to creatures with multiple sets of eyes.
These boots seem to disappear in shadows.

A Barbarian and a Wizard walked into a tavern; Most of the time, those stories don't end well. This was not one of those, well, except for the tavern...and the innkeeper...and the clients. At least the wine was good.
